{"key_event":"Demobilization, Prohibition, and Red Scare repression","reasoning":"After the WWI peak, federal economic controls receded under normalcy politics, but Prohibition, immigration quotas, the Budget Act, radio licensing, and federal policing kept state capacity very strong. Civil society remained strong and was broadened by women’s suffrage and Black institution-building, but labor, radical, immigrant, and pluralist organizations were weakened by repression, hostile courts, nativism, and employer power.","society_change":-0.1,"society_power":7.8,"state_change":-0.3,"state_power":8.6}
